speaker: seller

args.message: Welcome, welcome! Feast your eyes on this magnificent 2016 Chevrolet Tahoe LT. Perfect for the whole family and all your adventures, this beauty practically screams 'road trip'! With its spacious interior and powerful V8, you'll be cruising in style and comfort. Come on, let's talk turkey!

args.message: This Tahoe? Solid as a rock! No accidents to speak of, and she's been meticulously maintained by the dealer, just like the ad says. We actually just had her in for a full check-up – fresh oil, filters, the works. She runs like a dream, well, like a top-notch Tahoe! You can feel the quality the moment you get behind the wheel. Any other questions I can answer for you?

args.message: I appreciate that. A couple more things to nail down: What's the fuel economy like on the highway and in town? And can you give me a sense of the maintenance costs — oil changes, brake pads, that kind of thing — to help me budget going forward?

text: I appreciate that. A couple more things to nail down: What's the fuel economy like on the highway and in town? And can you give me a sense of the maintenance costs — oil changes, brake pads, that kind of thing — to help me budget going forward?

speaker: seller

args.message: Ah, the million-dollar question! For a full-size SUV like this beast, you're looking at around 16-18 MPG on the highway, and maybe 13-15 in the city. It’s pretty good for its class, especially with that powerful V8 engine. As for maintenance, these Tahoes are known for being pretty economical to keep running. Oil changes are standard, and brake jobs are infrequent, especially with how gently most owners treat these. Honestly, you’re looking at very reasonable costs for a vehicle of this caliber. It’s a workhorse that won’t break the bank!

args.message: Thanks for all that info. I'm interested in this Tahoe — it checks a lot of boxes for me. That said, at $24,900, it's a bit above where I'd like to be. I'd like to offer $22,800 to get us in the ballpark. What do you think?

speaker: seller

args.message: Fuel economy? For a robust V8 like this, you're looking at about 17 MPG on the highway and 14 MPG in the city. Pretty solid for a vehicle of this size! And maintenance costs are surprisingly reasonable. Regular oil changes and tire rotations are your mainstays. Brake pads last a good long while on these, and you won't be doing major work unless you're really pushing it. It's a dependable rig, built to last.

args.reason: $23,900 is still $1,900 above my hard budget of $22,000. I appreciate the seller coming down from the asking price, but I can't stretch beyond my limit. The Tahoe is a nice vehicle, but the price gap is too wide for me to make this work.
